correct_origin.title: Inference for Linear Conditional Moment Inequalities

wrong_credit.title: A More Credible Approach to Parallel Trends

chains.catalog_evidence: Conditional-CI machinery is Andrews-Roth-Pakes 2023 'Inference for Linear Conditional Moment Inequalities' (Review of Economic Studies, not Journal of Econometrics as earlier draft indicated). RR 2023 adopts it for honest-DiD inference under Delta-RM.

correct_origin.title: Optimal Inference in a Class of Regression Models

wrong_credit.title: A More Credible Approach to Parallel Trends

chains.catalog_evidence: FLCI machinery itself is Armstrong-Kolesar 2018 (Econometrica). RR 2023 adapts it for use with Delta-SD; cite both — RR for the honest-DiD application, Armstrong-Kolesar for FLCI methodology.

chains.method: not-yet-treated comparison group under conditional parallel trends

wrong_credit.title: Estimating dynamic treatment effects in event studies with heterogeneous treatment effects

chains.catalog_evidence: Sun-Abraham uses never-treated or last-to-be-treated as the comparison; the not-yet-treated comparison-group construction is CS 2021 Assumption 5.

wrong_credit.title: Estimating dynamic treatment effects in event studies with heterogeneous treatment effects

chains.catalog_evidence: Sun-Abraham's interaction-weighted estimator is NOT doubly-robust; the DR identifying functional with propensity-score and outcome-regression representations is CS 2021.

chains.method: sensitivity analysis under parallel-trends violations (Delta-RM, Delta-SD, breakdown M, FLCI, ARP)

correct_origin.title: A More Credible Approach to Parallel Trends

wrong_credit.title: Pretest with Caution: Event-Study Estimates after Testing for Parallel Trends

chains.catalog_evidence: Roth 2022 is the pretest-bias diagnostic critique; the constructive sensitivity framework with restriction sets on differential trends is Rambachan-Roth 2023.

chains.method: low power of pre-trends tests; conditional-on-pretest distortion

correct_origin.title: Pretest with Caution: Event-Study Estimates after Testing for Parallel Trends

wrong_credit.title: A More Credible Approach to Parallel Trends

chains.catalog_evidence: The pretest-bias and low-power diagnostic is Roth 2022 (AER:Insights). RR 2023 cites Roth 2022 but does not derive the diagnostic itself.

correct_origin.title: Estimation and Inference of Heterogeneous Treatment Effects using Random Forests

chains.catalog_evidence: Athey-Tibshirani-Wager 2019 Annals of Statistics is Generalized Random Forests (GRF), a broader framework. Causal forests proper is Wager-Athey 2018 JASA. The GRF paper builds on causal forests; it does not introduce them.
catalog_sources_of_claim: Athey, Tibshirani & Wager 2019 Annals of Statistics 47(2): 1148-1178
